mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in PAYEMS signals contracting aggregate wage income and weakening consumption demand, which reduces expected corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns after PAYEMS declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or if Rank IC > -0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 2.5%, it signals an aggressive easing cycle in response to financial stress, which compresses the equity risk premium and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 2.5%, or the mean return is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of declining retail sales (RSAFS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) jointly signals a consumption-demand collapse that is deeper than either signal alone, driving strongly negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS declines and UNRATE rises simultaneously, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over main effects.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-led downturn, a reading of HOUST below 1 million units triggers expectations of broader demand collapse, reducing earnings forecasts and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1000, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces aggregate demand expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when the UNRATE trigger is active, or the mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is below 3% and INDPRO declines month-over-month, the joint pattern indicates monetary easing is failing to stimulate supply, revealing underlying economic weakness that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between low FEDFUNDS and declining INDPRO does not significantly improve prediction (F-test p > 0.05) over individual main effects, or forward returns are positive in more than 45% of joint-trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in RSAFS signals weakening consumer spending that directly reduces top-line revenue expectations for consumer-facing S&P 500 constituents, producing negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between RSAFS changes and 20-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window, or negative returns occur in fewer than 55% of declining RSAFS months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in INDPRO signals expanding industrial capacity utilization and operating leverage, which boosts expected corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when INDPRO increases, or the mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).
